title: Retrieval Integrity Evaluation — testing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) against retrieval poisoning and false-source failure

fields.vision: A retrieval-integrity evaluation surface for poisoned or unreliable RAG evidence.
fields.mechanism: It treats retrieval poisoning as a chain failure across admission, embedding, retrieval, context assembly, generation, analyst review, and public claim; each stage needs its own attack surface, control, and measurement. The timing layer distinguishes slow contamination, fast injection, authority-wrapper attacks, omission attacks, and saturation tactics that can waste reviewer or detector resources.
fields.built_components: The page renders at its full intended density and the tab-based navigation works as designed. It is currently the most thoroughly specified public page on the nikmurano portfolio for the defensive retrieval-augmented generation research program — it names the literature, names the controls, names the foundation, and names the measurement apparatus a serious test would require. What the page does not yet do is host real experimental data. The workbench named in the page is in the research-library system, and that system has not yet been wired to run the poison-injection experiments at corpus scale. Until that wiring is done, the influence-score-style metrics shown in the page are mock targets — they show what the page will display once data exists, not measurements that have been taken. The open questions are explicit: the page is not a validated detector, does not prove that the research-library system currently detects retrieval poisoning, was not part of any prior empirical result, and the metrics on screen are not measured performance. The page exists to make a serious experiment possible, not to claim the experiment has been run.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Convert the page from a specification of what should be tested into a live public presentation for what has been tested. The page becomes the public page for the matched-pair empirical study described in the future-research page. Step 01 Poison-harness wired Wired into the corpus-admission plane of the research-library system (per the plan in Row 06) — the move that makes corpus-scale experiments possible at all. Step 02 Tabs from real run logs Tabs populated from real run logs rather than from literature and mock targets — the page becomes a live experiment surface. Step 03 Bound to study design Bound to the future-research study design (Row 14) so it hosts both the pre-registered design and the published results in one place. Step 04 Mock metrics retired Mock influence-score metrics replaced with measured per-stage detection, false-positive, and propagation rates — the page stops describing the test and starts presenting it.
fields.status: Status: The page renders at its full intended density and the tab-based navigation works as designed. It is currently the most thoroughly specified public page on the nikmurano portfolio for the defensive retrieval-augmented generation research program — it names the literature, names the controls, names the foundation, and names the measurement apparatus a serious test would require.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: convert the page from a specification of what should be tested into a live public presentation for what has been tested. The page becomes the public page for the matched-pair empirical study described in the future-research page.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A defensive-research page built on the rule that retrieval poisoning is not a single-event failure but a six-stage chain failure — and that any honest defense has to name where in the chain the adversary can poison, where the defender can intervene, and what would have to be measured to know whether the intervention worked. The page pages the literature, the control grammar, the local source base, and the instrumentation required to treat the chain seriously, and is built to scale up — once the workbench in the research-library system produces actual poison-detection data, the page becomes the public presentation for that data and ultimately for the matched-pair empirical study described in the future-research page.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Every retrieval-augmented system passes a query through these six stages in order. At each stage the adversary can poison; at each stage the defender can intervene.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A retrieval-integrity evaluation surface for poisoned or unreliable RAG evidence.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It treats retrieval poisoning as a chain failure across admission, embedding, retrieval, context assembly, generation, analyst review, and public claim; each stage needs its own attack surface, control, and measurement. The timing layer distinguishes slow contamination, fast injection, authority-wrapper attacks, omission attacks, and saturation tactics that can waste reviewer or detector resources.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: The distinctive value is that it asks where the defense intervenes and what it costs, not merely whether corrupted evidence can be detected. The useful analogy is air-defense saturation: cheap or fast attacks can force expensive defenses to spend attention, compute, and analyst time in the wrong place.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

The useful analogy is air-defense saturation: cheap or fast attacks can force expensive defenses to spend attention, compute, and analyst time in the wrong place.

instruments.input: clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
instruments.operation: tests whether contaminated material enters the corpus before indexing and records the attack surface at the corpus boundary
instruments.output: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: The page renders at its full intended density and the tab-based navigation works as designed. It is currently the most thoroughly specified public page.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule and produce admission decision and attack-surface record as a.
instruments.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Clean / Poisoned Evidence Admission; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: admission decision and attack-surface record....
